methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Standard Subsea, a seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Christopher Amidon, has raised $10M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of five private peers in the frontier ocean-tech sector (Clippership, NetworkOcean, Thalassa Robotics, Albacore, and Odin Dynamics), with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised of $10M as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $25M. This method reflects observed step-up patterns as companies transition from seed funding rounds.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. The relatively narrow band reflects the single-method foundation of this analysis. Key limitations include reliance on a small peer set and the absence of revenue, profitability, or operational metrics to validate stage-progression assumptions. The low end ($21M) and high end ($29M) bracket reasonable variance around the $10M capital proxy, accounting for execution risk and market conditions in the early-stage ocean-tech sector. Refinement of this range would benefit from additional stage-comparable transactions or revenue-based metrics if available.
